input: What were the findings regarding the variance components of SCYLV resistance traits in the cultivar panel studied in Guadeloupe?
expected_output: In the Guadeloupe cultivar panel, genetic and residual variances for SCYLV resistance traits were highly significant (P<0.0001). The ratio of genetic to residual variance ranged from 1.10 to 2.20 for stalk resistance and 2.53 to 4.40 for leaf resistance. Additionally, genotype-by-trial and genotype-by-crop-cycle interactions were highly significant.
context:
## Results  
Analysis ofSCYLV resistance traits inthe cultivar panel  
SCYLV resistance was assessed in the studied cultivar panel exposed to natural inoculation in the epidemiological context of Guadeloupe. Table1 summarizes the estimates of the variance components for both ANOVA model 1 (two trials together) and ANOVA model 2 (separate trial analyses). In model 1, variances of genotype×trial interaction (σ 2 GT ) and of genotype×crop cycles interactions (σ 2 GC ) were highly significant (P<0.001 or<0.0001) for all traits. input: How were SCYLV resistance alleles identified using phenotype data from trials in the study described in the text 'Association tests for SCYLV resistance'?
expected_output: SCYLV resistance alleles were identified by using phenotype data from each trial through GWAS approaches.
context:

input: What software and statistical methods were used to perform analyses on the phenotypic data described in the section 'Statistical analyses of phenotypic data'?
expected_output: The phenotypic data were analyzed using SAS software (version 9.3). Statistical methods included the arcsin transformation for virus incidence data, and analyses of variance (ANOVA) using mixed linear models (SAS Mixed procedure) with the restricted maximum likelihood (REML) method.
context:
## Statistical analyses ofphenotypic data  
All statistical analyses were conducted using the SAS software (SAS software 9.3, SAS Institute Inc., Cary, NC, USA). Virus incidence (VI) data were transformed by the ar sin () y
√VI
function before statistical analyses to achieve
l diibif idlIddf li normal distribution of residuals. Independency of cultivar means and variances were controlled for transformed virus incidence data and for virus population density data.  
Analyses of variance (ANOVA) were done for each disease resistance trait considering mixed linear models (SAS Mixed procedure) with the restricted maximum likelihood (REML) method. input: Which markers were significant in multiple trait analyses for leaf incidence using the Q1+K MLM model, and how many trait analyses were B424681 and B424690 significant in?
expected_output: Markers B424681 and B424690 were significant in five and four trait analyses, respectively, and both were significant for leaf incidence using the Q1+K MLM model.
